response:
```python
import pytest

# Update this import to match your module name
from your_module import is_valid_email


class TestIsValidEmail:
    @pytest.mark.parametrize(
        "email",
        [
            "user@example.com",
            "john.doe@example.org",
            "user123@test-domain.com",
            "name+tag@sub.example.co",
            "a_b.c-d%test@my-domain.net",
        ],
    )
    def test_valid_emails(self, email):
        assert is_valid_email(email) is True

    @pytest.mark.parametrize(
        "email",
        [
            "",  # empty string
            "plainaddress",
            "missingatsign.com",
            "@missinglocal.com",
            "missingdomain@",
            "user@domain",  # no TLD
            "user@domain.",  # trailing dot
            "user@.com",  # missing domain name
            "user@domain.c",  # TLD too short
            "user name@example.com",  # space in local part
            "user@exam ple.com",  # space in domain
            "user@@example.com",  # double @
            "user@example..com",  # invalid but regex may still accept some similar cases
            "user!@example.com",  # invalid character in local part
            "üser@example.com",  # non-ascii local part
            "user@example.c0m",  # numeric char in TLD
        ],
    )
    def test_invalid_emails(self, email):
        assert is_valid_email(email) is False

    def test_subdomain_email_is_valid(self):
        assert is_valid_email("user@mail.sub.example.com") is True

    def test_uppercase_email_is_valid(self):
        assert is_valid_email("USER@EXAMPLE.COM") is True

    def test_none_raises_type_error(self):
        with pytest.raises(TypeError):
            is_valid_email(None)

    def test_non_string_integer_raises_type_error(self):
        with pytest.raises(TypeError):
            is_valid_email(123)

    def test_non_string_list_raises_type_error(self):
        with pytest.raises(TypeError):
            is_valid_email(["user@example.com"])
```

Notes:
- Replace `from your_module import is_valid_email` with the actual module import.
- These tests cover:
  - happy path valid emails
  - edge cases like subdomains, uppercase, short TLD rejection
  - error cases for non-string inputs

One important note: the current regex will consider some technically questionable emails valid, such as domains with consecutive dots in some positions. The tests above focus on the behavior of the given implementation rather than full RFC-compliant email validation.
